author: Anita
review: I was a resident for 2 years at the start of the pandemic. I received my renewal offer going on the 2nd year and then a week later, my neighbor and I received a notice stating we were "selected" for a non renewal with no explanation. Please

ownerResponse.text:
Anita,
We are sorry to hear you were not happy with the service you received from our team.  We followed all legal processes during your tenancy and worked with you throughout the pandemic.  Our office hours did change during the pandemic and we shifted to working from home, however, all messages that you left were returned.  As far as maintenance goes, we do not mark up any invoices and during move-out situations we only charge for damages during tenancy, not for normal wear and tear.  We did look back at the maintenance requests you submitted during your tenancy and they were all completed in a timely manner.  We do wish you be best and hope you are satisfied with your next property management experience.

review: As a property owner, we have been using NW Property Management Group for close to 10 years.  In all honesty, we believe we get more than what we pay for.  From compliance to tenant screening to market knowledge, they are a tremendous

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the positive review.  We are happy to hear you are satisfied with our property management services.  It has been a pleasure working with you throughout the years.

review: I did a simple test with this company , i request a tour with them , they reply me with couple question to answer , i answered and i have a great credit score , they ignore my message and never replied me, then after 2 days , i made a fake

author: Tom Ross
review: Joseph and his staff are great to work with. Professional, knowledgeable and very helpful. I would highly recommend using their company for your property management needs!

author: Shelby Johnson
review: VERY poor management! I recently lived in an apartment that was managed by NWPMG and I was kicked out because I have a child.They would make up complaints of loud noises coming from my apartment when I would be on vacation and no one was

ownerResponse.text:
Shelby,
We are sorry you were not happy with the service you received from our company.  We certainly did not make up complaints about loud noises and you were not kicked out because you have a child.  A large portion of the people we rent to have children and have been renting from us for years.  We followed all legal processes during your tenancy.  We hope you have a good experience with the next property manager you work with.

author: Ashley Price
review: I most definitely wouldnt do business with them again or recommend them to any of my family or friends. All they care about is that you have the money to move in! So many red flags I should have paid closer attention to. They didnt even

ownerResponse.text:
Ashley,

We are sorry to hear about your experience with this house.  The home was professionally cleaned prior to you moving in.  In terms of the smell, I'm not sure what that would have been caused from.  The previous two tenants did not have a pet, however, this is why we do in person showings so that if there is something you don't like about the property there is no obligation to apply and pay an application fee.  Additionally, the property condition report provided to you upon move-in is a good way to document any of these items you mentioned.

We hope you found a place that you like and met your expectations.

Thank you,
NW Property Management Group

author: Maivi Nguyen
review: I've rented from a few rental properties for years and have taken tours of rental properties without issues...until today.

ownerResponse.text:
Maivi,
Thank you for your input about our showing process.  The purpose of having all potential tenants view the property is to make sure all tenants have a clear expectation of what the property looks like, location, etc. so we can avoid issues down the road.  Pictures do not tell the whole story.

As far as our showing schedule goes, we do allow our showing agent to have Sunday's off so she can spend time with her family.  I am sorry we were not able to accommodate your schedule but we hope you found a wonderful home.

Thank you,
NW Property Management Group

author: Lyn-Felice Calvin
review: I have worked with this company for two years. They communicate very well and try hard to provide good and clean properties for renters and owners. They know rental laws and fair pricing very well and they are really great to work with. They have always been responsive. I highly recommend this company.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the positive review.  We pride ourselves on providing great customer experiences and we are glad to hear you are happy with us.  Our team has enjoyed working with you over the last couple of years.

author: Alexis Fleming
review: The staff was always friendly but very unhelpful. Priscilla originally showed us the duplex and when we asked questions about the property the majority of her answers were "I don't know." We rented the duplex and had only been there a few

ownerResponse.text:
Alexis,

We understand your frustration with the property being sold and all that is involved with that as a renter.  Your cooperation with showings and inspections was greatly appreciated.  We were not aware of the owners intentions to sell this property when you moved in, nor do we have any control over those decisions.  We did inform the owners about people walking around your property and requested the agent put a "do not disturb" sign in the yard.  In terms of maintenance issues,  you mentioned there were several, however, we were only made aware of a couple outlets that were not working and we did not refuse to fix them. We were unable to obtain owner approval for the repair due to the pending sale and confusion between seller/buyer as to who would be responsible to pay for this.  Again we understand your frustration, however, these are situations where we do not have control over the decisions being made.  Thank you for your understanding.

The Team at NW Property Management Group

We enjoyed working with you and your family and appreciate your communication and respect for the property we manage.  We wish you the best in the future.  Take care.
The Team At NW Property Management Group

author: Grace Stewart
review: They don't answer the phone there. I had to give notice to move online, then could not get anyone to confirm they even recieved it for almost 2 weeks. When they finally got back to me, they said I would owe for another month, etc. etc.etc.

ownerResponse.text:
Grace,

We are sorry to hear you were not happy with our move-out process.  We take these experiences seriously and use them as an opportunity to improve our systems and processes.  In doing so I would like to address your concerns specifically.  Our phone system is automated and often times our office does get busy, however, if you leave a message we do return calls in a timely manner.

The lease agreement does indicate that notice to terminate tenancy must be given in writing so providing this online was appropriate on your part.  In doing some investigating we found that we did respond to you two days after you gave your notice as our move-out team sent you an email which included the move-out guidelines.

Additionally, there is no question that you were a good tenant and we appreciate the time you spent in the house.  We wish you the best and hope you are happy in your new home.

Thank you,
Northwest Property Management Group

author: Grace Shin
review: My husband and I had arranged an appointment to see a property. He had called and set everything up Monday morning and we set up a time for the upcoming Saturday morning. As the week rolled closer to Saturday, I found it strange that we

author: Kelsie R
review: DO NOT RECOMMEND. This business is the worst business I have ever dealt with. They have very little ways to communicate with them. This is a huge problem because this is where we LIVE. We need to be able to communicate with our property

ownerResponse.text: We are sorry to hear you feel we are difficult to reach.  We did send out communication to all our tenants when the stay at home order went into effect letting everyone know that we would be working remotely and some response times may take longer.  That being said, our team has no voice messages or emails from you that have not been responded to.  The phones at our office have been checked for messages at least twice a week and there have been none from anyone in your unit.  Additionally, our records show that all maintenance requests have been responded to in a timely manner.  We do take your feedback seriously and will discuss your concerns with our team.  Thank you!

author: Oscar Rubalcaba
review: THESE PEOPLE DO NOT CARE ABOUT YOU!  They are incredibly difficult to reach, via email or phone! And when they do, the time of reply is only on their convenience. When there is maintenance to be done they will not want to do that, or allow

ownerResponse.text:
Oscar,

We are sorry to hear you feel we are difficult to reach.  We did send out communication to all our tenants when the stay at home order went into effect letting everyone know that we would be working remotely and some response times may take longer.  That being said, our team has no voice messages or emails from you that have not been responded to.  The phones at our office have been checked for messages at least twice a week and there have been none from anyone in your unit.  Additionally, The last maintenance request was in 2018 and our records show that all maintenance requests have been responded to in a timely manner.  If you have a maintenance emergency we have a 24 hour emergency line you can call.  This number can be found in all of our voicemails and emails.  Further, it looks like you make your rent payment online through your tenant portal.  These payments are posted to your tenant ledger right away, however, it does take about 3 banking days to notify us if a payment has been returned.  There are no late fees that have been posted to your account.  We hope this information helps to clarify any confusion or miscommunication.

author: Jenna Noble
review: I have a service animal and I was trying to rent a place from them but they were doing everything in their power to deny us because of the service animal's breed.  Every time they would bring up an issue with our situation, I would find a

ownerResponse.text:

I'm sorry to hear about the experience you had with the property management company.  When I went to research what went wrong during your application process we were unable to find any record of an application with your name in our system.  There are quite a few property  management companies with similar names in the area.  Is it possible you have us confused with another company?  We do not deny a person's application due to a service animal.

author: Caroline Capoun
review: I had reached out to this company on the week of 02-10-2020 about two properties my roommates and I wished to view. Through the week I remained in contact and let them know we had chosen one of the two properties to view and that our

ownerResponse.text:
Caroline,

While we are not required to put the lease terms in our ads, we do understand there was miscommunication with this particular house and we apologize for that.  We have discussed this as a team and appreciate you bringing this to our attention so we can improve our process going forward.  Good luck with your new home search.

NW Property Management Group

author: LaurieA. Shriver
review: SCAM ALERT! I just read through most of these reviews & they're going to SCAM YOU OUT OF MOST OF YOUR DEPOSIT as NW Properties did to me and a ton of other former tenants (below)!! I gave three months warning that I had to leave the unit

ownerResponse.text:
Laurie,
We are sorry to hear how disappointed you were with your move-out process and expenses.  Our standard process is to clearly communicate the move out process with our tenants when they give notice.  As our team indicated to you when you gave your notice to move, you were breaking your lease which causes additional expenses so this should not have been a surprise to you.  When a lease expires, this is something that is usually charged to the owner. Due to the lease not being fulfilled, this becomes your charge per RCW 58.18.310.

(b) When the tenancy is for a term greater than month-to-month, the tenant shall be liable for the lesser of the following:
(i) The entire rent due for the remainder of the term; or
(ii) All rent accrued during the period reasonably necessary to re-rent the premises at a fair rental, plus the difference between such fair rental and the rent agreed to in the prior agreement, plus actual costs incurred by the landlord in re-renting the premises together with statutory court costs and reasonable attorneys' fees.

Please note the lease break fee does not only pay to run the ad but for our administrative costs to have someone answer inquiry's about the ad, show the property, and process applications.  Had you fulfilled the term of your lease you would not have been responsible for this cost.

It is not our intention or our business practice to take advantage of you, or any tenant, in any way.  You were fully aware of the consequences of breaking your lease.

We wish you the best in your future endeavors.

author: Abbigayle Rahman
review: Horrible business! We are currently preparing to move cross country, and when reaching out to view homes to sign a lease for one month prior. They informed me that they wont let me schedule anything until Im ready to move in 2 weeks

ownerResponse.text:
Abbigayle,

I would like to clarify our policy to clear up any confusion. We do require all financially responsible party's view the property prior to processing their applications.  Additionally, we require that you take financial responsibility within 7 to 14 days of submitting your application as we do not hold properties.  This has been our standard practice for over 11 years.  It is our job to get properties rented as quickly as possible and provide outstanding customer service to our owners and tenants.

We do update our website on a regular basis so you can always check back and see what we have available closer to when you are able to move.

Thank you,
NW Property Management Team

review: They never came out to fix anything that was wrong with the house when we moved in, even after repeat maintenance calls and two years. Charged an arm and a leg for maintenance and fees on move out. During the inspection before move in

ownerResponse.text: Since we cannot find your name in our system we are unable to look in to this further to see where improvements can be made.

author: Ann Chesterman
review: We are a military family. Nothing was ever fixed. And we were kicked out when my husband was gone on deployment. Because the landlord wanted to sell the homes we lived in for 4 years. In witch was not up for sale in the first place. And

ownerResponse.text: Ann, We honor and appreciate your family's service to the military.  When you move in to a rental home you must expect that at some point the owner may want to sell the home or move back in.  You were provided with adequate notice per Washington State Law.  I'm sorry this happened to coincide with your husbands deployment, however, the owner was experiencing some hardships and were left with no other choice.  If it was possible, they would have had you continue to rent, however that was not the case.  Further, there were no outstanding work orders submitted by you, therefore we are not aware of any issues that were not fixed.

author: Brittaney Thomas
review: Reached out to request information and was rudely blown off via email by Priscilla. She failed to even try to assist me and because I told her my credit was "less than 600 she dismissed me immediately. She became passive aggressive and

ownerResponse.text:
Brittaney,

We are sorry to hear you did not have a good experience with our company and would like to clarify the information Priscilla was trying to communicate.  When one aspect of the qualifying criteria is a bit weaker we do take a stronger look at other aspects of the qualifying criteria, and an overall assessment of the facts and circumstances in an effort to get an application approved.  Sometimes it's hard to determine without processing the application and running a credit report.  Because there is a $55 application fee that cannot be refunded once we pull credit we will sometimes give the applicant an option to move forward if the outcome is uncertain.  That way we are not wasting an applicants money unnecessarily.  We are sorry this information was not communicated in a more constructive way.  We do appreciate your comments so that we can improve on our process.

NW Property  Management Group Team

review: Was in contact with a landlord of a property with this group,arranged a showing,the landlord NEVER SHOWES UP,DID NOT ANSWER ANY CALLS,TEXTS.....FAKE...FAKE....

ownerResponse.text: Dalena,  I think there may be a misunderstanding.  I can see from our rental inquiry inbox that there is email correspondence between you and our showing agent. A showing appointment was scheduled for 3:30pm, however, our showing agent was there until 3:50pm and they did not show up at any time during at time frame. We take your feedback seriously and look for ways to improve based on that.  Thank you.  NWPMG

author: Harold Cox
review: This company does not care about any of its tenants is job is to get your money.

ownerResponse.text: Howard, it is not true that we do not care about our tenants.  When a tenant signs a lease agreement they are agreeing to certain terms and conditions and our expectation is that the agreement will be followed.  It is our responsibility as a property manager to collect rent and any money owed for damages to an owner's property beyond normal wear and tear.

author: Jr Huey
review: Very good people that help a lot

ownerResponse.text: Thank you!

author: Taylor Souders
review: Awful about responding for maintenance requests. Will never rent from them in the future.

author: Randy Ross
review: Best property management company around.
